approval_rule: A group recommendation never grants approval; every approved or blocked artefact must retain a checksum-bound human decision.

allowed_fields: project-owned source, schemas, tests, docs and automation
excluded_fields: third-party data, descriptors, ontologies, trademarks and provider documentation
groups.provenance_required: Preserve third-party notices and source-specific terms.
groups.recommended_outcome: Yes, for material the project owns or is authorized to license.
groups.simple_question: May project-owned code and documentation be distributed under Apache-2.0?
groups.title: Project code and documentation

groups.provenance_required: Provider URL, release ID, retrieval timestamp, SHA-256, parser version, output checksum and attribution.
groups.recommended_outcome: Publish only permitted derived fields with attribution; retain raw XML/TXT locally and review descriptor-only rows separately.
groups.simple_question: May the specified derived MBS fields be redistributed under the applicable Commonwealth terms?
groups.title: Australian MBS

groups.provenance_required: Endpoint or catalogue URL, key provenance without the secret, retrieval timestamp, checksum, parser version and source terms.
groups.recommended_outcome: Publish reviewed derived schedule/list or payment values with PBS attribution; never publish raw responses, headers or credentials.
groups.simple_question: May the selected PBS schedule, item and fee fields be redistributed?
groups.title: Australian PBS

groups.provenance_required: Exact CMS URL, release ID, access date, checksum, permitted-field decision, parser version, excluded-field record and attribution.
groups.recommended_outcome: Permit only reviewed numeric/payment fields and permitted metadata; exclude CPT descriptors, restricted crosswalks and unsupported coverage or net-price claims.
groups.simple_question: Which numeric CMS payment fields may be retained and published without redistributing restricted descriptors?
groups.title: US CMS CLFS, PFS and ASP

groups.provenance_required: Official publication URL, file checksum, directory version, parser version, field exclusions and attribution.
groups.recommended_outcome: Publish only reviewed directory metadata and preserve provider attribution and version identifiers.
groups.simple_question: May transformed genomic-directory metadata be redistributed under NHS terms?
groups.title: NHS England genomic directories

groups.provenance_required: Repository commit, source IDs, input checksums, transformation version, output checksum and licence decision.
groups.recommended_outcome: Publish project-owned artefacts under Apache-2.0 or an explicitly recorded compatible metadata licence, while preserving embedded source restrictions.
groups.simple_question: May project-generated provenance, quality, protocol, dictionary and research-package artefacts be published?
groups.title: Generated research and governance artefacts

source_catalogue_scope: Counts are historical_source_catalogue records only; a zero count means the source is governed elsewhere and is not evidence of source absence.
